repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest API entry is ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study, a static bounded-outcome catalogue entry

published_prior_art.cite: Robins1986 g-formula endpoint; DiemerShiSwanson2024 sustained-strategy assumption-free bounds; KimKwonKwonLee2018 smoothness-bound nesting

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 concerns dynamic IV memory, pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 concerns surrogate drift, pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 concerns DID anticipation boxes

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest are KimKwonKwonLee2018 static smoothness bounds, JunLeeShin2016 stationarity panel bounds, ChernozhukovFernandezValHahnNewey2013 time-homogeneity panel bounds
BojinovRambachanShephard2021 dynamic path effects and Han2021 dynamic treatment identification are orthogonal, but the Cesaro/Bellman limit is largely standard once Conjecture 1 is granted

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the draft gives plausible comparators but no cited paper leaves this reversible-spell Lipschitz sharpness regime open, so the current kernel reads as a new sensitivity construction rather than a flagship-eligible strict extension or regime opening.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Corollary zero
soundness_flags.one_line: The rho=0 reduction does not recover the standard Robins g-formula: because the current-treatment mismatch is multiplied by rho, rho=0 forces all observed support outcome regressions at a state to be equal, and the envelope becomes max(mu) versus min(mu) unless that strong no-effect condition holds.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The Lipschitz restriction is stated only for prefixes 'on the reversible support' while the bound is applied to unobserved sustained-history nodes; the proposal must say whether the restriction is global over all treatment prefixes or only observed-support, because the latter would make the proposed interval too tight.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The Robins transition kernel is treated as known at sustained-regime histories, but the positivity assumption only guarantees a nonempty observed path set, not positive support for the target action at each recursive history; off-support covariate transitions need positivity or their own residual bounds.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Separate the current-treatment effect from the carryover radius, for example with a metric or restriction that lets rho=0 mean no adjacent-spell contamination while retaining ordinary Robins treatment effects under sequential exchangeability.

Add an explicit transition-support condition for every sustained-regime history used in the g-recursion, or broaden the partial-ID functional to include bounded uncertainty over off-support covariate transitions.

To aim for flagship tier, cite a specific published open problem or theorem class strictly extended by the reversible-spell Lipschitz construction, and state the strict-containing class in observable-law terms.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version and redraft the endpoint convention and transition-positivity substrate before re-running novelty review.
